_meta.description: JSON patch for M300 grammar gap analysis — 2026-03-30
_meta.instructions: Apply these changes to modernization-ledger.json and initialization.json

M300.title: Close remaining COBOL-85 grammar gaps (36 verified remaining)
M300.description: Grammar audit verified 2026-03-30: of 138 COBOL-85 gaps identified, ~102 are fixed. 36 verified remaining: Data Division 15 open, Procedure Division 10 open, Environment Division 11 open, Expressions 0 open, Lexer Tokens 0 open. Gaps categorized as 12 REJECT (valid programs rejected, high priority), 15 OVERLENIENT (accepts invalid COBOL, low priority), 9 SEMANTIC-ONLY (handled by M302/M304/M310).

SEMANTIC_ONLY.note: Not grammar fixes; handled by existing items

batch_1.name: Quick wins
batch_1.description: USE GLOBAL/EXCEPTION/modes, STOP RUN STATUS, START WITH LENGTH, ALPHABET FOR, CLASS FOR
batch_1.nist_unblocked: 6+ NC tests, IC suite (47), IX suite (42)

batch_2.name: Medium complexity
batch_2.description: SET ON/OFF, SORT Format 2, SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS N:N

batch_3.name: Architectural (deferred)
batch_3.description: CURRENCY WITH PICTURE SYMBOL (PICMODE refactor), SCREEN SECTION (entire new section)

deliverables: Close all 12 REJECT gaps (M400-M411)
Close all 15 OVERLENIENT gaps (M412-M426)
Add positive and negative parser tests for each fix
Update GRAMMAR_AUDIT.md to reflect 0 open COBOL-85 items
Run guard.sh after each batch
risks: Grammar changes can break existing 95 NC + 421 unit + 274 integration tests
PICMODE lexer architecture blocks CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL (M407)
SCREEN SECTION (M411) is an entire new grammar section — large scope
test_impact: Parser unit tests for each new/modified construct
Regression runs of all NC and future non-NC NIST suites
guard.sh must pass after each batch
acceptance_criteria: All 12 REJECT gaps have grammar rules that parse valid COBOL-85
All 15 OVERLENIENT gaps reject invalid COBOL-85 with proper diagnostics

M302.description: The grammar audit notes 5 not-implemented Data Division COBOL-85 items. M300 grammar verification (2026-03-30) confirmed 9 SEMANTIC-ONLY gaps overlap with this item: REDEFINES level validation (01/66/77/88 rejection), RENAMES level-66 enforcement, INITIALIZE category keyword completeness (5 of 13), VALUE clause Format 2 table init, PIC editing validation.
M302.audit_evidence: M300 grammar verification 2026-03-30: confirmed 5 Data Division semantic gaps are grammar-correct but validation-missing. These are not grammar fixes.

M304.description: The audit notes REDEFINES 7-rule constraint enforcement is unclear, and NC252A shows 3 FAIL*. M300 grammar verification (2026-03-30) confirmed: grammar allows REDEFINES on any level (should reject 01/66/77/88 per §13.18.44 SR1). This is a SEMANTIC-ONLY gap — grammar accepts the syntax, but SemanticAnalyzer must validate level constraints.
M304.audit_evidence: M300 grammar verification 2026-03-30: REDEFINES level validation confirmed as semantic-only gap. Grammar correctly parses REDEFINES; validation must enforce §13.18.44 SR1.

M310.description: PERFORM Range overlap detection (§14.8.35) — Not implemented. M300 grammar verification (2026-03-30) confirmed: this is a SEMANTIC-ONLY gap. Grammar correctly parses PERFORM ranges; ControlFlowBinder must implement overlap detection.
M310.audit_evidence: M300 grammar verification 2026-03-30: PERFORM range overlap confirmed as semantic-only gap. Grammar is correct; binder must validate.

reject_gaps.title: SET switch-setting ON/OFF (8 SET formats absent)
reject_gaps.description: SET TO ON / TO OFF (switch-setting) format is missing from grammar. 8+ SET formats absent per §14.9.39. Valid COBOL-85 programs using SET my-switch TO ON are rejected at parse time.
deliverables: Add SET switch-setting grammar rules to CobolParserCore.g4
Add parser unit tests for SET ON/OFF forms
Add integration tests for switch-setting behavior
risks: SET statement has 8+ formats — must not break existing SET forms (index, boolean, address, value)
Implementor-name resolution requires semantic layer coordination
test_impact: Parser unit tests for SET switch forms

reject_gaps.title: START WITH LENGTH phrase
reject_gaps.description: START WITH LENGTH integer phrase is missing from CobolIO.g4 per §14.9.41. Valid COBOL-85 programs using partial-key matching via START file-id WITH LENGTH are rejected.
deliverables: Add WITH LENGTH phrase to startStatement in CobolIO.g4
Add parser unit tests
Add integration test for partial-key START
risks: Interaction with existing START KEY IS forms must be preserved

reject_gaps.title: SORT Format 2 (table sort)
reject_gaps.description: SORT Format 2 (table sort — SORT table-name ON KEY) is missing from CobolIO.g4 per §14.9.40.2. Valid COBOL-85 programs using in-memory table sorting are rejected.
deliverables: Add SORT Format 2 grammar rules to CobolIO.g4
Add parser unit tests for table SORT
Add integration test for in-memory table sorting
risks: Must disambiguate from SORT Format 1 (file sort) at grammar level
Requires SortRuntime extension for in-memory sort

reject_gaps.title: USE GLOBAL keyword
reject_gaps.description: USE AFTER EXCEPTION GLOBAL keyword is missing from CobolControlFlow.g4 per §14.9.49. Valid COBOL-85 programs with nested program exception scope using USE AFTER EXCEPTION GLOBAL are rejected.
deliverables: Add GLOBAL keyword option to useStatement in CobolControlFlow.g4
Add parser unit tests
Add integration test for GLOBAL exception handling in nested programs
risks: Nested program exception propagation semantics must be correct

reject_gaps.title: USE EXCEPTION/ERROR synonym
reject_gaps.description: USE AFTER ERROR form (synonym for EXCEPTION) is missing from CobolControlFlow.g4 per §14.9.49. Valid COBOL-85 programs using ERROR instead of EXCEPTION are rejected.
deliverables: Add ERROR as synonym for EXCEPTION in useStatement
Add parser unit tests for both forms
risks: Minimal — simple synonym addition

reject_gaps.title: USE INPUT/OUTPUT/I-O/EXTEND modes
reject_gaps.description: USE AFTER EXCEPTION ON INPUT/OUTPUT/I-O/EXTEND mode keywords are missing from CobolControlFlow.g4 per §14.9.49. Valid COBOL-85 programs specifying file operation modes for exception handling are rejected.
deliverables: Add INPUT/OUTPUT/I-O/EXTEND mode options to useStatement
Add parser unit tests for each mode
Add integration test for mode-specific USE declaratives
risks: Must correctly distinguish file-name reference from mode keyword

reject_gaps.title: STOP RUN WITH ERROR/NORMAL STATUS
reject_gaps.description: STOP RUN WITH ERROR STATUS / WITH NORMAL STATUS phrase is missing from CobolControlFlow.g4 per §14.9.42. Valid COBOL-85 programs specifying exit status are rejected.
deliverables: Add WITH ERROR/NORMAL STATUS phrase to stopStatement
Add parser unit tests
Add integration test verifying exit code propagation
risks: Runtime must propagate status to .NET process exit code

reject_gaps.title: CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL
reject_gaps.description: CURRENCY SIGN IS literal WITH PICTURE SYMBOL literal phrase is missing from CobolSpecialNames.g4 per §12.3.7.1. Explicitly noted as omitted in code comment at CobolSpecialNames.g4:47-48. Blocked by PICMODE lexer architecture — the lexer pushes a special mode when PIC keyword is detected, preventing complex phrase parsing in environment clauses.
deliverables: Refactor PICMODE lexer handling to allow post-PIC-string clauses
Add CURRENCY SIGN WITH PICTURE SYMBOL grammar rule
Add parser and integration tests for multi-currency programs
risks: PICMODE lexer refactoring may break existing PIC string parsing
Financial/currency programs produce incorrect formatted output without this

reject_gaps.title: SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS N:N mapping
reject_gaps.description: SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS clause is limited to 1:1 mapping (one name per entry). Spec §12.3.7 allows multiple names per entry: SYMBOLIC CHARACTERS sym-1 sym-2 ARE int-1 int-2 IN alphabet-name. Also missing IN alphabet-name phrase.
deliverables: Extend symbolicCharactersClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4 for N:N mapping
Add IN alphabet-name optional phrase
Add parser unit tests for multi-name entries
risks: Semantic layer must resolve N:N ordinal-to-character mapping

reject_gaps.title: ALPHABET FOR ALPHANUMERIC/NATIONAL phrases
reject_gaps.description: ALPHABET clause is missing FOR ALPHANUMERIC / FOR NATIONAL phrases per §12.3.7.2. Valid COBOL-85 programs specifying alphabet scope are rejected.
deliverables: Add FOR ALPHANUMERIC/NATIONAL option to alphabetClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add parser unit tests
risks: Semantic layer must route alphabet to correct character set

reject_gaps.title: CLASS FOR ALPHANUMERIC/NATIONAL phrases
reject_gaps.description: CLASS definition clause is missing FOR ALPHANUMERIC / FOR NATIONAL phrases per §12.3.7.2. Valid COBOL-85 programs specifying class scope are rejected.
deliverables: Add FOR ALPHANUMERIC/NATIONAL option to classDefinitionClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add parser unit tests
risks: Semantic layer must validate class condition against correct character set

reject_gaps.title: SCREEN SECTION
reject_gaps.description: SCREEN SECTION is completely missing from CobolData.g4 per §13.4. This is an entire grammar section with its own data description entries, ACCEPT/DISPLAY semantics, and screen field attributes (BLANK, BELL, BLINK, HIGHLIGHT, etc.).
deliverables: Add screenSection grammar rules to CobolData.g4
Add screen data description entries with all attributes
Add parser unit tests for screen definitions
Semantic layer: screen field binding and validation
risks: Large scope — entire new section with ~20 clauses
Requires ACCEPT/DISPLAY statement extensions for screen I/O
Runtime must support terminal interaction (may need Console abstraction)

overlenient_gaps.title: OCCURS TIMES keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: occursClause has timesKeyword? but spec §13.18.38 requires TIMES keyword. OCCURS 5 accepted without TIMES.
deliverables: Remove ? from timesKeyword in CobolData.g4
Add negative parser test rejecting OCCURS without TIMES
risks: Existing COBOL programs using non-standard OCCURS 5 form will break

overlenient_gaps.title: OCCURS INDEXED BY keyword required (BY currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: occursClause has INDEXED BY? but spec §13.18.38 requires BY keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from BY in INDEXED phrase in CobolData.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard INDEXED without BY will break

overlenient_gaps.title: OCCURS KEY IS keywords required (both currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: occursKeyClause has KEY? IS? but spec §13.18.38 requires both KEY and IS keywords.
deliverables: Remove ? from KEY and IS in occursKeyClause in CobolData.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard ASCENDING without KEY IS will break

overlenient_gaps.title: LINAGE IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: linageClause has LINAGE IS? but spec §13.18.34 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in LINAGE clause in CobolData.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard LINAGE without IS will break

overlenient_gaps.title: BLANK WHEN ZERO — WHEN keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: blankWhenZeroClause has BLANK WHEN? ZERO but spec §13.18.9 requires WHEN keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from WHEN in blankWhenZeroClause in CobolData.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard BLANK ZERO will break

overlenient_gaps.title: ORGANIZATION IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: organizationClause has ORGANIZATION IS? but spec §12.4.5.3 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in organizationClause in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard ORGANIZATION SEQUENTIAL will break

overlenient_gaps.title: ACCESS MODE IS keywords required (both currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: accessModeClause has ACCESS MODE? IS? but spec §12.4.5.4 requires both MODE and IS keywords.
deliverables: Remove ? from MODE and IS in accessModeClause in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard ACCESS RANDOM will break

overlenient_gaps.title: RELATIVE KEY IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: relativeKeyClause has RELATIVE KEY IS? but spec §12.4.5.2 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in relativeKeyClause in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard RELATIVE KEY identifier will break

overlenient_gaps.title: ALTERNATE RECORD KEY — RECORD keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: alternateKeyClause has ALTERNATE RECORD? but spec §12.4.5.6 requires RECORD keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from RECORD in alternateKeyClause in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard ALTERNATE KEY will break

overlenient_gaps.title: ALTERNATE WITH DUPLICATES — WITH keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: alternateKeyClause has WITH? DUPLICATES but spec §12.4.5.6 requires WITH keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from WITH in alternateKeyClause in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard DUPLICATES will break

overlenient_gaps.title: COLLATING SEQUENCE IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: sortCollatingPhrase has COLLATING SEQUENCE IS? but spec §14.9.40 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in sortCollatingPhrase in CobolIO.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard COLLATING SEQUENCE alphabet will break

overlenient_gaps.title: CURRENCY SIGN IS — SIGN keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: currencySignClause has CURRENCY SIGN? IS? but spec §12.3.6.3 requires SIGN keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from SIGN in currencySignClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard CURRENCY IS will break

overlenient_gaps.title: CURRENCY SIGN IS — IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: currencySignClause has CURRENCY SIGN? IS? but spec §12.3.6.3 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in currencySignClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard CURRENCY SIGN literal will break

overlenient_gaps.title: CLASS name IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: classDefinitionClause has IS? but spec §12.3.7.2 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in classDefinitionClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard CLASS name values will break

overlenient_gaps.title: ALPHABET name IS keyword required (currently optional)
overlenient_gaps.description: Grammar accepts invalid COBOL-85: alphabetClause has IS? but spec §12.3.7.2 requires IS keyword.
deliverables: Remove ? from IS in alphabetClause in CobolSpecialNames.g4
Add negative parser test
risks: Existing programs using non-standard ALPHABET name values will break
